channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: India emphasises holistic peace and maritime security at BRICS foreign ministers' meeting
item.description: During the opening session of the two-day BRICS foreign ministers' conclave, External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar highlighted India's serious concerns over ongoing tensions in West Asia, particular...

item.title: Ewan McGregor’s Early Career on the Set of Shallow Grave (1994)
item.description: In 1994, a young Ewan McGregor, at just 22 years old, starred in 'Shallow Grave,' a film that marked the beginning of a key creative partnership with director Danny Boyle and producer Andrew Macdonald...

item.title: STAMICO and Plantcor Launch Kigosi Gold Mining Project in Geita Region
item.description: Tanzania’s State Mining Corporation (STAMICO) has joined hands with South Africa’s Plantcor Mining and Plant Hire to develop the Kigosi Gold Mining Project in Bukombe District, Geita Region. The partn...

item.title: Elon Musk and Sam Altman face legal scrutiny over OpenAI's for-profit conversion
item.description: The trial between Elon Musk and OpenAI, including CEO Sam Altman, is reaching its final stages in Oakland, California. Musk accuses OpenAI of breaching its nonprofit mission and enriching executives a...

item.title: Punjab Police Plans to Take Custody of Arrested Drug Suspect from Sindh
item.description: LAHORE: Following the arrest of Anmol alias Pinky by Sindh Police in Karachi, Punjab Police are now seeking her custody for questioning in connection with three old drug-related cases registered in La...

item.title: Mbappé Returns From Injury As Real Madrid Defeat Oviedo 2-0
item.description: Kylian Mbappé don return from him thigh injury as he come on as substitute for Real Madrid for their La Liga match against Real Oviedo. Fans for Santiago Bernabéu stadium no too happy with am, dem eve...

item.title: Four spectators removed from Eurovision semi-final following disruptions during Israel's act
item.description: During the first semi-final of Eurovision 2026 in Vienna, four audience members were ejected for disrupting Israel's performance. Israeli singer Noam Bettan began performing 'Michelle' when a proteste...

item.title: A Tribute to Liliane Montevecchi: Icon of Stage and Screen
item.description: Liliane Montevecchi (1932–2018) was an extraordinary performer whose career spanned both European cabaret and Hollywood cinema. Beginning as a prima ballerina, she quickly became a star at Paris’s leg...

item.title: San Beda and Mapua to Compete in Philippine Youth Basketball Championship U18
item.description: San Beda University and Mapua University are set to participate in the Philippine Youth Basketball Championship (PYBC) Under-18 division. The tournament, which aims to develop young basketball talents...

item.title: Analysis of President Trump's Recent Social Media Activity and Its Implications
item.description: Over a roughly 21-hour period from Monday night into Tuesday morning, President Donald Trump posted or reposted 77 times on his Truth Social account, with more than 50 posts occurring in just over an ...

item.title: P.E.I. advocates welcome new sexual orientation question in 2026 census for better visibility and funding
item.description: For the first time in Canadian history, the 2026 census includes a question on sexual orientation, marking a significant step towards greater recognition of 2SLGBTQ+ communities. While Statistics Cana...

item.title: A Detailed Look at the 1960 Jaguar XK150 Drophead Coupe
item.description: The 1960 Jaguar XK150 Drophead Coupe represents the final evolution of Jaguar's iconic XK series, combining elegance, performance, and innovation. Introduced as the most refined grand tourer of its er...

item.title: Mamelodi Sundowns at Risk of First Trophyless Season in Over a Decade
item.description: Mamelodi Sundowns, historically dominant in South African football, are facing the possibility of a rare trophyless season for the first time in 13 years. Coach Miguel Cardoso is under increasing pres...

item.title: PM Modi Embarks on UAE and Europe Tour to Discuss Energy and Technology Partnerships Amid Iran Conflict
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has planned an official visit to the United Arab Emirates followed by a tour of four European nations, aimed at strengthening bilateral cooperation in energy and technolog...

item.title: Senate Parliamentarian Blocks Republican Immigration Funding Proposal
item.description: Republicans faced a major setback in the Senate as Parliamentarian Elizabeth MacDonough blocked key parts of a proposed immigration enforcement funding package. The package, aimed at fully funding Imm...

item.title: Chef Ben Shewry criticises food media after initial praise
item.description: Ben Shewry, the acclaimed chef behind Melbourne's renowned Attica restaurant, has publicly criticised the food media industry after years of receiving glowing reviews. Initially celebrated by critics ...

item.title: Key Updates: First Nations Concern Over Major Projects, Hantavirus Cases, and Political Developments in Canada
item.description: On May 14, 2026, several important developments unfolded across Canada. The Assembly of First Nations (AFN) national chief, Cindy Woodhouse Nepinak, criticized the federal government’s proposed change...

item.title: Legal Challenges Delay Alberta’s Proposed Separation Referendum Over Indigenous Consultation
item.description: Alberta’s proposed referendum on separation from Canada has been temporarily derailed following a court decision focused on Indigenous consultation. Justice Shaina Leonard of the Court of King’s Bench...

item.title: Montreal Victoire edges Ottawa Charge 3-2 in overtime to take first Walter Cup final game
item.description: In an exciting start to the Walter Cup finals, the Montreal Victoire secured a narrow 3-2 overtime victory against the Ottawa Charge in Game 1. The match was tightly contested, with both teams display...

item.title: South African mining growth slows amid global uncertainty and rising costs
item.description: South Africa’s mining sector experienced a slowdown in March, with production rising only 2.5% year-on-year, falling short of the 4.1% expected and well below February’s revised 9.7% increase. Statist...
